data.four_set_effect: When a character attacks an opponent affected by Cryo, their CRIT Rate is increased by 20%. If the opponent is Frozen, CRIT Rate is increased by an additional 20%.

circlet_of_logos.desc: The crown of an ancient hero who dreamt of conquering the cold. It is proof of its former master's bravery in facing the bone-chilling cold of winter.
circlet_of_logos.title: Broken Rime's Echo

flower_of_life.desc: A long-extinct flower, covered in beads of frost, that once grew on the glaciers. There was a time when even the proudest and most arrogant warriors bowed before it.
flower_of_life.title: Snowswept Memory

goblet_of_eonothem.desc: A cup carved out of ice that is as chilly and biting as winter. Its former master would drink an unfreezable liquor from it.
goblet_of_eonothem.title: Frost-Weaved Dignity

plume_of_death.desc: A feather that exudes a chilly aura. One can almost feel the turbulent winds that brought it here, wailing over snow-covered plains and between frosty peaks.
plume_of_death.title: Icebreaker's Resolve

sands_of_eon.desc: A timepiece from a nation of old that waited for their warriors' return. What flows inside is not sand, but bits of ice that never melt.
sands_of_eon.title: Frozen Homeland's Demise

list.desc: This is a flower of ever-frost, plucked by a pair of gentle hands.</br>It must have given a certain someone the strength to see winter as a warm embrace.</br>"This fourth fresco is prepared for you. Your likeness will forever remain on this wall."</br>"For the sake of that fresco, and for everyone's sake, I will wait for you, and pray for your return..."</br>The maiden stood before a blank wall, and smiling, she pinned a small flower to the hero's breast.</br>That was an elegant and unruffled fellow, who would never bat an eyelid, not even in the face of death itself.</br>The heroism of the Icebreaker was forgotten, buried in the storms of the North.</br>Since then, the snow has melted — but this flower has not withered.</br>
list.title: Snowswept Memory

list.desc: The hero set out on his journey, bearing the tiny hopes that the entombed city still had for salvation.</br>Donning his winter crown, he marched purposefully away, disappearing into the boundless snowstorm.</br>Bearing his agreement with the mountain city, bearing their clear-eyed gazes, the hero never once feared the unknown beyond the icy curtain.</br>The once-lush mountainside and the blessings that now no longer fell from the skies — these were the hero's motivation to carry on no matter what.</br>"Past the ice-sealed door, walking down the corridors to the depths,"</br>"He will break off a branch of silver-white, and bring hope to the snowy land."</br>The maiden sang these songs to comfort her people, and she nursed her memories of him.</br>She believed that he would return to her, with genial springtime and unshakeable hope in his train.</br>But in the end, the hero who stepped out into the snow would not return in time.</br>He would stand accused of flight by hateful words long ground to dust amid the snowstorms...</br>
list.title: Broken Rime's Echo

list.desc: For the outlander who once searched for hope in midwinter on another's behalf,</br>Even the glass he drank from was carved out of ice.</br>Drinking bitter wine in this glass would feel like swallowing icicles that stung every inch of one's throat.</br>Ordinary people would struggle to enjoy this sensation — but the reticent hero relished it.</br>He was a warrior as silent as the ice itself, blocking the frigid winds that descended from the stars with his body.</br>But not content with merely being protected, the maiden who painted left the man she admired these last instructions:</br>"If fear or despair, both of which are within our nature, should crush you, or cause you to never return, then..."</br>"...Then please, live on. Do not accompany us unto our doom — to languish forgotten in the snow."</br>Taking another swig of bitter wine to steel himself for the journey, he turned away from the maiden's wet eyes,</br>And he would set out on an unending pursuit, towards the land of snow and profundity.</br>
list.title: Frost-Weaved Dignity

list.desc: A feather of a bird of prey that did not originally belong in the winter chill. It is cold to the touch.</br>And when you do touch it, it is as if you can hear cries in the snowstorm, unbowed but bereft of hope.</br>With no nest or shelter, the bird faced the storms proudly.</br>This feather has been assailed with coalescing ice and snow, leaving it coated in pearls of frost.</br>It once came from a falcon in flight and was plucked violently from that bird by the frigid winds.</br>It drifted a while in the air before the weight of the frost dragged it back down to the ground.</br>"I believe that the lively birds will follow you, and return to the gardens of the summer palace, now green once more."</br>"Those who have been chased away by the chilling tide, and those children who have lost their homes, they will return with you to the home of their dreams."</br>The hero, bearing such great burdens on his back, halted in the whipping snow, struggling to discern the color of that feather.</br>Sealed in ice, that feather's color faded every step of the way — just like the hero's task.</br>
list.title: Icebreaker's Resolve

list.desc: An ancient hourglass with fine powdered snow flowing in it.</br>Even the coldest winter cannot freeze the flow of time.</br>"The cold that descends from the sky can freeze time itself."</br>This myth was widely believed throughout the entombed mountain city.</br>As the hero reached the top of the wall of wind and ice, the night was descending and the snowstorm was howling.</br>Neither sun- nor moon-light could pierce this white wind easily.</br>Even the bleakest storm cannot freeze the river of time,</br></br>That will not wilt even if buried deep below the snow.</br>And even the most formidable hero will one day be forgotten.</br></br>
list.title: Frozen Homeland's Demise
